Computer Science.saturation_note: CS is crowded at the entry level, so a real portfolio and internship experience matter more than CGPA alone.
Computer Science.reality_note: Competitive field. Top graduates with strong portfolios and internships can earn well, but weak coding fundamentals make entry-level hiring difficult.

Software Engineering.saturation_note: Software engineering rewards skill quickly, but fresh graduates without projects often get filtered out early.
Software Engineering.reality_note: Good career path if the student builds real projects. Degree name matters less than problem-solving ability, GitHub work, internships, and communication skills.

Artificial Intelligence.saturation_note: AI has hype and opportunity, but real junior AI roles are fewer than software roles and require stronger math and deployment skills.
Artificial Intelligence.reality_note: High upside but fewer true entry-level AI roles. Many jobs still require strong software engineering, math, Python, and real model deployment experience.

Data Science.saturation_note: Data science is competitive because many applicants know tools, but fewer can solve messy business problems with clean analysis.
Data Science.reality_note: Growing field, but many Pakistani companies still have messy data systems. Students need SQL, statistics, Python, dashboards, and business understanding to stand out.

Electrical Engineering.saturation_note: Electrical engineering has solid sectors, but graduate supply is high and many students shift toward software or telecom for better starts.
Electrical Engineering.reality_note: Traditional engineering hiring is slower than IT. Better outcomes usually come from power sector roles, embedded systems, telecom, automation, or shifting into software.

Mechanical Engineering.saturation_note: Mechanical engineering is respected but crowded, and the better early roles usually require plant exposure, CAD skills, and internships.
Mechanical Engineering.reality_note: Respectable degree but local industry absorbs graduates slowly. Strong CAD, manufacturing exposure, internships, and willingness to work on-site matter a lot.

Civil Engineering.saturation_note: Civil engineering depends heavily on construction cycles, so fresh graduates often face low starting salaries and site-heavy work.
Civil Engineering.reality_note: Early salaries can be low and site work is demanding. Career growth depends heavily on construction cycles, project experience, software skills, and professional networking.

BBA / Business Administration.saturation_note: BBA is very saturated unless the student has strong communication, internships, Excel skills, and a clear specialization.
BBA / Business Administration.reality_note: Very broad degree with mixed outcomes. Graduates from top schools do much better; others need internships, English communication, Excel, analytics, and sales confidence.

Accounting & Finance.saturation_note: Finance has steady demand, but the best roles usually go to students with CA, ACCA, CFA progress, or strong analytics skills.
Accounting & Finance.reality_note: More stable than many business degrees, but growth is stronger with ACCA, CA, CFA progress, Excel, Power BI, and practical accounting software experience.

Psychology.saturation_note: Psychology is growing, but decent income often requires supervised practice, specialization, and trust-building over time.
Psychology.reality_note: Mental health awareness is rising, but income can be modest without further specialization, supervised clinical hours, private practice, or organizational psychology experience.

Pharmacy.saturation_note: Pharmacy has many graduates chasing limited attractive roles, so hospital, industry, and regulatory paths are more valuable than basic retail jobs.
Pharmacy.reality_note: Stable healthcare-related path, but retail and entry-level roles often pay less than students expect. Better outcomes come from hospital, industry, regulatory, or multinational roles.

MBBS / Medicine.saturation_note: MBBS has high job security, but it requires 5 years plus house job before earning well and specialization is often necessary.
MBBS / Medicine.reality_note: Strong long-term profession but the path is long, stressful, and expensive. Early earnings after graduation can be modest until specialization, residency, or private practice develops.
